title: Quantifying antibody binding to Fcγ-receptors in malaria immunity
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"elwkt","text":"IgG can opsonise pathogens and mediate binding to Fcγ-receptors (FcγRs) expressed by various innate immune cells to promote antibody-mediated pathogen clearance via mechanisms including opsonic phagocytosis and antibody-dependent cellular cytotoxicity (ADCC). High levels of IgG that bind FcγRs has been associated with protection from malaria. This assay measures FcγR-binding by antigen-specific IgG using recombinant FcγRs in a plate-based method which is suitable for high throughput. The method is suitable for using recombinant proteins or whole malaria parasites in the assays. FcγRIIa and FcγRIIIa/b are low affinity receptors and need to be used as ectodomain dimers in assays in order to achieve good activity in the assays. FcγRI can be used as a monomeric protein.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"prn85","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"pynj2","text":"Reagents and equipment:","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":23}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3ghub","text":"High-binding flat bottom plate (e.g. 96-well flat bottom Maxisorp plates (NUNC Thermo Scientific #442404)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5kh7v","text":"1% BSA in 1X PBS: dissolve 10 g of bovine serum albumin (BSA) in 1L 1X phosphate-buffered saline (PBS), stir at room temperature (RT) until completely dissolved. Filter-sterilise and store at 4°C.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1vbeu","text":"Antigen","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7nfco","text":"37°C incubator","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cn9f3","text":"PBS-Tween 0.05%: 1X PBS with 0.05% Tween-20 (v/v)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ar88k","text":"FcγRI conjugated to biotin (from commercial suppliers); FcγRIIa or FcγRIIIa conjugated to biotin (expressed and purified in-house at Burnet Institute)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"danol","text":"Streptavidin conjugated horseradish-peroxidase (HRP) (e.g. Pierce High Sensitivity Streptavidin-HRP (Thermo Scientific #21130))","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"54dbs","text":"Substrate (TMB (Life technologies #00-2023) OR ABTS (Life technologies #00-2024))","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6h5jh","text":"Sulfuric acid (if using TMB) OR SDS (if using ABTS)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7gqdk","text":"Spectrophotometer (e.g. Multiskan GO Microplate Spectrophotometer (Thermo Scientific))","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Coat wells with 50 µL antigen (1 µg/mL). Incubate overnight at 4°C. (Assays can also be performed coating wells with whole parasites, including merozoites, sporozoites, or gametocytes/gametes)
Discard supernatant. Wash well 4 times with PBS-Tween 0.05%. Discard any remaining wash buffer from wells.
Block plate with 200 μL of 1% BSA in PBS and incubate at 37°C for 2 hours.

Add 50 µL of test serum/antibody, diluted in 1% BSA in PBS (optimisation of concentration may be necessary), to each well and incubate either for 1 hour at 37°C, for 2 hours at room temperature or overnight at 4°C.

Add 50 µL of FcγRIIa (0.2 ug/mL) or 50 µL of FcγRIIIa (0.1 ug/mL) or FcγRI (0.125 μg/ml), diluted in 1% BSA-PBS. Incubate at 37°C for 1 hour. NOTE – concentrations of FcγRs used may need to be optimised for the specific study purpose.
Add 50 µL of streptavidin-HRP, diluted 1:10,000 in 1% BSA in PBS, and incubate at 37°C for 1 hour.
Add 50 µL of substrate (TMB or ABTS) to each well. Incubate in the dark at room temperature while signal develops (usually 10 – 60 minutes).

Stop reaction by adding 50 µL of stopping solution (1M sulfuric acid for TMB, 1% SDS for ABTS) to each well. Read optical density immediately after stopping. TMB absorbance is measured at 450 nm, while ABTS absorbance is measured at 405 nm.
creator: Protocols Importer
